Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Same Hevea source; Figure 2D shows predominant expression in latex.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: HRBP is explicitly identified as an NgBR homologue and higher-plant orthologue with one predicted N-terminal transmembrane domain.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Y2H shows interactions with HRT1 and REF and HRBP self-interaction, but not HRT2; BiFC additionally detected HRT2-HRBP interaction. The field accurately notes HRT2 as BiFC-only/not Y2H-supported.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used in Results for HRBP: RP proteomics, qRT-PCR, Y2H, BiFC, localization/co-expression in N. benthamiana, co-IP, WRP reconstitution, and RTase assay.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: HRBP:mTq2 localized mainly to ER with small dots; co-expression moved HRBP to Golgi with HRT1 and to plasma membrane with HRT2; ternary complex with HRT1 and REF localized mainly on ER.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Figure 8 shows HRBP expression escalated WRP coagulation, and text states HRBP alone did not activate background RTase activity.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking application inference. The paper suggests utility for reconstitution/enhancement, but does not directly test breeding or engineering outcomes.

Omics_Data.reason: Proteomics on detergent-washed RPs was performed; HRBP accession LC057267 is given; transcriptome reference PRJNA82895 appears in datasets/methods.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: HRBP was studied under CHAPS detergent-washed RP conditions and cell-free translation-coupled introduction onto WRPs.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Natural rubber is presented as an industrial rubber/elastomer material throughout the paper context.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper supports HRBP as a bridging/supportive factor rather than catalytic enzyme and states NgBR family involvement enhances efficient assembly/stability but is not strictly necessary for catalytic activity on WRPs.

Applied_Species.reason: REF studied in Para rubber tree/Hevea brasiliensis latex and WRPs.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experiments were performed in H. brasiliensis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: REF in this paper is from H. brasiliensis.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show REF decreases WRP coagulation, stabilizes particles, and markedly enhances HRT1-HRBP RTase activity as part of the ternary complex.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: REF is linked to natural rubber biosynthesis/RTase machinery in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: REF is a rubber particle-bound protein in latex.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each statement is supported by Results: REF interacts with HRBP and relocalizes HRT1-HRBP to ER, co-IPs with HRBP and HRT1/HRT2, and boosts HRT1 activity with HRBP while stabilizing WRPs.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Although detailed REF expression data are cited from prior work rather than newly shown, the paper explicitly states REF is RP-abundant and notes predominant latex expression from Aoki et al. 2014a.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Same as applied species; REF is described as abundant/predominant in latex context.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: REF is explicitly called rubber elongation factor.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Y2H shows REF-HRBP, REF-REF, and REF-SRPP interactions; HRT1/HRT2 association is supported indirectly through ternary complex/co-IP rather than direct Y2H.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in the Results for REF, except 'proteomics context' is somewhat indirect but acceptable because REF was intentionally removed before LC-MS/MS and discussed as abundant RP protein. The rest are directly used.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: REF localizes mainly to ER; BiFC signals with partners appear on specific particles/aggregates.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper shows REF alone does not activate RTase and its effect is mainly in combination with HRT1 and HRBP.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Application value is inferential. The paper suggests future utility but does not directly test engineering outcomes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Natural rubber is an industrial elastomer/biopolymer in context.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The study presents direct functional evidence that REF is a module of the RTase machinery and stabilizes WRPs.

Enzyme_Name.reason: HRT1 is described as a cPT that exhibits RTase activity on WRPs; thus 'rubber transferase / cis-prenyltransferase' is supported.

Applied_Species.reason: HRT1 was tested using Hevea rubber particles from Para rubber tree.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experimental system uses H. brasiliensis WRPs.

Source_Species.reason: HRT1 is from Para rubber tree/Hevea brasiliensis.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Figure 9 shows HRT1 introduction onto WRPs distinctly increases RTase activity; HRBP enhances it and REF further activates it.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: HRT1 is directly linked to natural rubber synthesis in vitro.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Natural rubber is stored in latex rubber particles; HRT1 activity was reconstituted on WRPs.

Primary_Product.reason: GPC showed high-molecular-weight products with size distributions similar to endogenous natural rubber; described as cis-1,4-polyisoprene/natural rubber.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper proposes HRT1 on WRP catalyzes de novo rubber formation using allylic primers and IPP via sequential cis-condensation.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: HRT1 is studied as part of natural rubber biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: HRT1 performs cis-polyisoprene elongation on rubber particles, as described.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: HRT1 is the catalytic component of RTase making the terminal polymer product.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: HRT1 directly produces rubber/polyisoprenoid product in the assays.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: HRT1 is explicitly a member of the cis-prenyltransferase family.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly supported by Figures 7-9 and discussion anchored in Results.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Same Hevea source; latex-predominant expression is stated.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: HRT1 localizes to Golgi and weak ER with HRBP, shifts to ER with HRBP plus REF, and is introduced onto WRPs in vitro.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are used for HRT1 in Results.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: No activity on liposomes and increased WRP coagulation with HRT1 expression unless REF is present are both reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable inference but not directly tested in breeding/engineering experiments.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: CHAPS-washed RPs, wheat germ cell-free expression, and allylic primer dependence are explicit experimental factors.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Natural rubber's industrial material role is implicit from paper context.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly argues HRT1 may be more important than HRT2 for NR biosynthesis while not excluding HRT2 involvement.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The Hevea WRP-based reconstitution system and FPP addition are explicit.
